overexcitement.description: Bauschinger's clean mechanistic structure (kinematic hardening, back-stress, cyclic plasticity) feels like it should give predictive purchase on RLHF asymmetric softening; that aesthetic preference pulled me toward the candidate beyond what the prior-art landscape supports.

honest_note: Half my forced hits are materials-science source-domain pages (Wikipedia, model papers, NN-for-Bauschinger). The substantive prior art on the actual LLM phenomenon is so thoroughly covered — arxiv 2504.09757 explicitly names 'aligned direction vs harmful direction' asymmetry, 2502.17424 documents narrow-finetuning→broad-misalignment, 2602.21420 names 'Asymmetric Confidence Penalties', 2502.11555 'Equilibrate RLHF' covers safety-helpfulness oscillation — that even with a clean Bauschinger framing the contribution would be a labeling and tighter quantitative theory, not a new finding. Consistent with saturation_evidence: cross-domain analogy + LLM/RLHF problem area is saturated to a depth where novel mechanism imports become labels rather than discoveries.
